title: T9: Kill fab_plan_context degenerate mode
description: Q-12 resolved: `fab_plan_context` degenerate mode (when candidates ≤ 30, return `candidates_full_content` directly) silently bypasses `knowledge_consumed` event emission and breaks rc.5 C5 closure. Remove the degenerate-mode branch in `plan-context.ts`: always return `description_index` + `selection_token` regardless of candidate count. Agent must follow up with `fab_get_knowledge_sections` (which DOES emit `knowledge_consumed`). Update tool-contracts snapshot tests to reflect symmetric output. Record rationale in `docs/decisions/rc5-a3-superseded.md`. Side effect: SessionStart hook footer 'Use `fab_get_knowledge_sections` to fetch full content' becomes accurate again (was misleading at ≤30 entries when full content was already inlined).

files.change: remove the `if (candidates.length <= 30) { return candidates_full_content }` branch; always return { description_index, selection_token, total_candidates }; ensure selection_token TTL/storage path still works at all sizes

update snapshot: small-candidate-set fixture now returns description_index shape (NOT candidates_full_content); add explicit negative test asserting candidates_full_content field does not appear in any response
(verify no change needed) confirm knowledge_consumed event still emits per-section fetch — this becomes the sole consumption-signal path post-T9

create new ADR: title 'rc.5 A3 fab_plan_context degenerate mode superseded by rc.7 T9'; body documents rationale: closes knowledge_consumed silent bypass + improves Agent context economy (don't pre-load content user might not need) + API symmetry (single response shape regardless of size)
ensure SessionStart footer 'Use `fab_get_knowledge_sections` to fetch full content' is unconditional (no special-case wording for small repos)
add assertion: invoke fab_plan_context with 5 candidates AND 100 candidates → both responses contain `description_index` + `selection_token` AND neither contains `candidates_full_content`

implementation: Locate the degenerate-mode branch in plan-context.ts; verify it's clearly isolated (likely an early-return at top of handler)
Remove the branch; ensure description_index generation works for small N (no division-by-zero or similar)
Update unit tests: replace the small-N expectation with description_index shape; add explicit negative assertion `expect(response).not.toHaveProperty('candidates_full_content')`
Write the ADR: 1-page document explaining what was, what is, why; cross-reference rc.5 A3 ticket if it exists
Verify fab_get_knowledge_sections still emits knowledge_consumed event correctly (check existing tests; no code change expected)
Update SessionStart footer wording for symmetry
Coverage gate: programmatic check at both candidate-set sizes

reference.pattern: rc.5 A3 design — degenerate mode was an early optimization; rc.7 reverts for closure correctness
reference.examples: ADR style: status / context / decision / consequences

criteria: GIVEN canonical/ contains 5 entries WHEN fab_plan_context is called THEN response contains `description_index` and `selection_token` AND does NOT contain `candidates_full_content`
GIVEN canonical/ contains 100 entries WHEN fab_plan_context is called THEN response has the same shape (description_index + selection_token)
GIVEN Agent calls fab_plan_context then fab_get_knowledge_sections with selection_token THEN events.jsonl contains a `knowledge_consumed` event for the second call
All plan-context contract tests pass with the new symmetric snapshot

rationale.chosen_approach: Hard removal of degenerate mode — symmetry over micro-optimization; consumption signal is the hill to die on
alternatives_considered: Keep degenerate mode but emit knowledge_consumed inline (breaks separation of concerns)
Make degenerate mode opt-in via param
Lower threshold to ≤5 instead of ≤30 (postpones problem)
decision_factors: rc.5 C5 closure requires reliable knowledge_consumed signal
Agent context economy (don't load N entries if Agent needs 2)
API symmetry simplifies all downstream consumers
rationale.tradeoffs: Agent must do two MCP calls instead of one for small canonical sets — accepted, fab_get_knowledge_sections is cheap and selection_token amortizes

manual_checks: Run fab_plan_context with small canonical (5 entries) → inspect response shape
Run with large canonical (100 entries) → confirm identical shape
Verify events.jsonl gets knowledge_consumed only via fab_get_knowledge_sections path
success_metrics: Zero occurrences of candidates_full_content in any fab_plan_context response
knowledge_consumed event emission rate ≥ 95% of fab_get_knowledge_sections calls
unit: Contract test: response shape symmetric across candidate counts
integration: Two-call flow: plan_context → get_knowledge_sections → verify events.jsonl knowledge_consumed entry
